title: Salt
pov_character: Unnamed antagonist operative (cold open); Kirin Saleh (primary)
summary: Kirin walks out of Kesmark, the last Compact settlement, and learns that the empty zone is hunted, that the maintenance grid is curated rather than autonomous, and that her grandmother was active in the corridor in the months before her death. She finds a body at mile twenty-two and takes from it a small chit that is a cousin to her own key. She reaches a way-station called Salt, kept by Naila Brace, a fifties ex-Free Zone medic who charges in salt because salt cannot be faked. There she meets Dez, who is not a wise sage but a working operator with her own interests; survives a quiet confrontation with a planted thief named Bex; and discovers in Naila's salt-cured ledger that Adaeze Okonkwo passed through Salt in September 2125, six months before her death, paying three pounds of salt to confirm whether the Curator was still working his old route. When a comm broadcast on a dead frequency speaks Kirin's name, Naila puts her in a vehicle with Dez and sends them east on a maintenance road that does not appear on any Compact map.
locations: Unmapped maintenance road east of Salt
themes: objects that talk to each other
continuity_notes: MAJOR REWRITE supersedes v1 The Frontier script. Adaeze passed through Salt in September 2125 paying three pounds of salt for one ledger question. Maintenance grid is curated by at least three factions; one led by the Curator. Bex is a Curator operative. Kirin took a second chit from a dead woman named Renata at mile 22. The key now warms and cools in handshake patterns near curated infrastructure. Dez is a morally ambiguous operator who drives Kirin east toward Yusra at Larch depot. Naila stays at Salt to lie to the Curator and buy three days.
